page.title: Marbella Boat Rental in June: Weather, Sea Conditions & Tips

data.meta_description: Discover marbella boat rental weather june conditions: 25°C, 21°C sea temp, 9h sun, calm seas. Book your charter in Puerto Banús with skipper & drinks included.
data.summary: June in Marbella offers near-perfect boating weather: 25°C air, 21°C sea, 9 hours of sun, and calm Mediterranean seas. This guide covers sea conditions, wind patterns, booking tips, and the best boats for June charters from Puerto Banús.
key_facts: Average air temperature in June: 25°C (highs up to 29°C).
Sea temperature reaches 21°C – comfortable for swimming.
9 hours of daily sunshine, sunset after 21:30.
Typical wind: 10-15 knots from the west, seas calm.
Water visibility often exceeds 15 metres in June.
June is peak season – book 2-3 weeks ahead for best availability.
All our charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and VAT.
Puerto Banús departures: 15+ boats from 6.4m to 29m.

faq.q: What is the weather like in Marbella in June for boat rental?
faq.a: June in Marbella brings warm, sunny days with average highs of 29°C and lows around 19°C. Sea temperature is 21°C, ideal for swimming. Expect 9 hours of direct sun and very little rain (only 3-4 rainy days historically). Winds are mild at 10-15 knots, making for calm sailing and motor cruising.

faq.q: Do I need a jacket for a June boat charter in Marbella?
faq.a: Probably not during the day, but bring a light windbreaker or long sleeve for the evening return trip. After sunset, the sea breeze can drop temperature to around 19°C, and at 25 knots of boat speed it feels cooler. Our boats have sheltered flybridges or cabins if you need a break from the wind.

faq.q: Is June a good month for swimming from a boat in Marbella?
faq.a: Yes – the sea temperature averages 21°C in June, which most people find comfortable for a dip. By late June it can reach 23°C. Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, and the coves near Cabopino offer clear, calm waters. We include towels on all charters.

faq.q: How far in advance should I book a boat in Marbella for June?
faq.a: June is the start of peak season. We recommend booking 2-3 weeks ahead, especially for weekends and popular boats like the Mangusta 80 (24m) or Azimut 58 Flybridge. Last-minute availability is possible on weekdays, but choice will be limited. Contact us via WhatsApp for real-time availability.

faq.q: Which boats are best for a June charter in Marbella?
faq.a: For a group of 9-12, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) offers space, speed, and a free jet ski. For a smaller group, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) at €749/2h is great value. The Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) provides shaded upper deck – perfect for June sun. All depart from Puerto Banús.

faq.q: What time of day is best for a June boat trip in Marbella?
faq.a: A 10:00-18:00 charter gives you the warmest sea and maximum sun. For sunset lovers, a 16:00-21:00 slot lets you watch the sun set behind La Concha while enjoying calm evening seas. Sunset is around 21:40 in June. Both options work well – just avoid the 13:00-15:00 peak heat if you prefer shade.

faq.q: Are there any weather risks for boating in Marbella in June?
faq.a: June is statistically the safest month for boating on the Costa del Sol. The Levante wind (easterly) is rare; Poniente (westerly) at 10-15 knots is typical. Thunderstorms occur on fewer than 2 days per month. Our skippers monitor forecasts and will adjust the route if needed – safety is always the priority.

faq.q: What is included in the price of a June boat rental in Marbella?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish VAT (21%). For Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) the price is clear: €749/2h to €2,299/8h. For other boats, request pricing via WhatsApp.

data.body_html:
June is arguably the sweet spot for a marbella boat rental weather june conditions: warm air, pleasant sea temperatures, long sunny days, and minimal wind. With average highs of 29°C, sea temperatures of 21°C, and 9 hours of daily sunshine, the Mediterranean off Puerto Banús is at its most inviting. Whether you're planning a family day out, a group celebration, or a romantic sunset cruise, this guide covers everything you need to know – from sea conditions and wind patterns to booking tips and the best boats for the month.

June Weather Averages for Marbella

June in Marbella delivers consistently good weather. Historical data shows average daytime highs of 29°C, with overnight lows around 19°C. Sea temperature reaches 21°C by mid-month – warm enough for extended swimming without a wetsuit. Rainfall is minimal (3-4 days total), and humidity is moderate at 60-65%. The sun rises around 07:00 and sets after 21:30, giving you over 14 hours of daylight.

For boaters, the key metric is wind. The prevailing Poniente (westerly) wind blows at 10-15 knots in June, creating a gentle chop of 0.3-0.6m in the open sea but calm conditions close to shore. The Levante (easterly) is rare in June, meaning you're unlikely to encounter the stronger winds that can affect autumn charters. Water visibility often exceeds 15 metres, especially near the coves east of Marbella.

Sea Conditions & Best Times to Go

The Mediterranean along the Costa del Sol is generally calm in June. The coastline from Puerto Banús to Estepona offers sheltered waters, while heading east towards Cabopino and Cala del Faro gives you slightly more exposed but still manageable conditions. Our skippers know the local microclimates – they'll adjust the route based on the day's forecast.

For the smoothest ride, morning charters (10:00-14:00) typically have the calmest seas. Afternoons can see a light thermal breeze pick up, but it rarely exceeds 15 knots. Sunset charters (16:00-21:00) are popular for the golden light and cooler temperatures – the sea often remains flat until dusk.

What to Wear & Pack for a June Boat Charter

Daytime: swimwear, shorts, t-shirt, sunglasses, and a hat. Sunscreen SPF 50 is essential – the reflection off the water intensifies UV exposure. We provide towels on all charters. For the evening return, pack a light jumper or windbreaker as the temperature can drop to 19°C after sunset, and the boat's movement creates a wind chill. Non-slip shoes are recommended but not mandatory – barefoot is fine on deck.

Don't forget a camera or phone for the views of La Concha mountain and the Marbella skyline. If you're prone to seasickness, consider taking a preventative antihistamine an hour before departure – though with June's calm seas, it's rarely needed.

Best Boats for June Charters

Our fleet of 15+ boats departing from Puerto Banús includes options for every group size and budget. For June, we recommend:

  • Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) – Tier A pricing: €749/2h to €2,299/8h. Great value, spacious cockpit, and a bow sundeck for soaking up the June sun.
  • Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) – The flybridge offers shaded seating for those who want to escape the midday heat while still enjoying panoramic views.
  • Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) – Available in grey and white variants. Minimum 4h at €4,719, includes a free jet ski – perfect for June water sports.
  • Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) – Our largest yacht, ideal for luxury celebrations with ample indoor and outdoor space.
  • Speedboat (7m, 6 pax) – For a more adventurous, wind-in-your-hair experience. Priced on request.

All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks (water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava), light snacks, insurance, and Spanish VAT. No hidden fees.

Itinerary Ideas for June

With 9 hours of daylight, you can tailor your route. Popular June itineraries include:

  • Puerto Banús to Cala del Faro (east): 30 minutes from base. Swim in turquoise waters, then anchor near the cliffs for lunch. Return via the Marbella coastline.
  • Puerto Banús to Estepona (west): 45 minutes. Stop at the fishing port for fresh seafood, then explore the quieter coves near Bahía de Estepona.
  • Sunset cruise to Cabopino: Depart at 17:00, cruise east, drop anchor for a swim, then watch the sun set behind La Concha while enjoying cava.
  • Full-day to Sotogrande: 1.5 hours each way. Visit the upscale marina, have lunch at a beach club, and return via the coast.

Your skipper will advise on the best option based on wind and sea conditions on the day.

Booking Tips for June

June is the start of the high season in Marbella. We recommend booking 2-3 weeks in advance, especially for weekends and popular boats like the Mangusta 80 or Azimut 58. Weekday availability is generally better. For the best value, consider a 4-hour charter (€1,499 for Tier A boats) which gives you enough time for a swim stop and coastal cruise without committing to a full day.

All our boats depart from Puerto Banús, easily accessible by taxi from Marbella centre (10 minutes) or Málaga airport (45 minutes). Parking is available nearby. Contact us via WhatsApp for real-time availability and pricing on boats not listed in Tier A or B – we'll respond within minutes.

Frequently asked questions

What is the weather like in Marbella in June for boat rental?

June in Marbella brings warm, sunny days with average highs of 29°C and lows around 19°C. Sea temperature is 21°C, ideal for swimming. Expect 9 hours of direct sun and very little rain (only 3-4 rainy days historically). Winds are mild at 10-15 knots, making for calm sailing and motor cruising.

Do I need a jacket for a June boat charter in Marbella?

Probably not during the day, but bring a light windbreaker or long sleeve for the evening return trip. After sunset, the sea breeze can drop temperature to around 19°C, and at 25 knots of boat speed it feels cooler. Our boats have sheltered flybridges or cabins if you need a break from the wind.

Is June a good month for swimming from a boat in Marbella?

Yes – the sea temperature averages 21°C in June, which most people find comfortable for a dip. By late June it can reach 23°C. Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, and the coves near Cabopino offer clear, calm waters. We include towels on all charters.

How far in advance should I book a boat in Marbella for June?

June is the start of peak season. We recommend booking 2-3 weeks ahead, especially for weekends and popular boats like the Mangusta 80 (24m) or Azimut 58 Flybridge. Last-minute availability is possible on weekdays, but choice will be limited. Contact us via WhatsApp for real-time availability.

Which boats are best for a June charter in Marbella?

For a group of 9-12, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) offers space, speed, and a free jet ski. For a smaller group, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) at €749/2h is great value. The Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) provides shaded upper deck – perfect for June sun. All depart from Puerto Banús.

What time of day is best for a June boat trip in Marbella?

A 10:00-18:00 charter gives you the warmest sea and maximum sun. For sunset lovers, a 16:00-21:00 slot lets you watch the sun set behind La Concha while enjoying calm evening seas. Sunset is around 21:40 in June. Both options work well – just avoid the 13:00-15:00 peak heat if you prefer shade.

Are there any weather risks for boating in Marbella in June?

June is statistically the safest month for boating on the Costa del Sol. The Levante wind (easterly) is rare; Poniente (westerly) at 10-15 knots is typical. Thunderstorms occur on fewer than 2 days per month. Our skippers monitor forecasts and will adjust the route if needed – safety is always the priority.

What is included in the price of a June boat rental in Marbella?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish VAT (21%). For Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) the price is clear: €749/2h to €2,299/8h. For other boats, request pricing via WhatsApp.
